name: South Sinai Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the field of the flag and the snow-capped peaks of the Sinai mountains
the granite mountains of southern Sinai, including Mount Sinai (Jabal Musa) and Mount Catherine, the highest peak in Egypt
the Red Sea coast of southern Sinai and elements of the emblem
the desert of Sinai and the historic monastic and Bedouin heritage
details and inscriptions of the emblem

description: A white field charged in the centre with the emblem of South Sinai Governorate, depicting the grey mountains of southern Sinai with red, yellow and black detailing.
history: The flag of South Sinai Governorate features its mountain emblem on a white field, adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s. South Sinai includes Mount Sinai (Jabal Musa) and the Saint Catherine's Monastery.

features.symbolism: the granite mountains of South Sinai, including Mount Sinai and Saint Catherine, central to the governorate's identity
the emblem of South Sinai with its mountains, Bedouin and monastic heritage, and Red Sea coast
